frameworks.name: active inference under the free-energy principle
frameworks.one_line: agents minimize free-energy by adjusting internal states and sampling of the environment

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: reward/value-based optimization of behaviour the paper argues is unnecessary

frameworks.name: dynamic programming / optimal control
frameworks.one_line: value-iteration control framework benchmarked against free-energy formulation

frameworks.name: predictive coding / perception-action unification
frameworks.one_line: free-energy formulation extended to jointly explain perception and action

core_question: Can adaptive goal-directed behaviour be produced without invoking reward, value, or utility, using only free-energy minimization?
key_assumptions: agents act to minimize free-energy of sensory exchanges with environment
perception and action share a single objective
causal structure of the environment is learnable in self-supervised fashion
no explicit reward signal is required
manipulations: replacing reward-based optimization with free-energy minimization
applying active inference to the mountain-car benchmark
contrasting resulting policy with dynamic-programming solution
key_findings: agent learns environmental causal structure via free-energy minimization
behavioural policies reproduce those obtained by reinforcement learning and dynamic programming
mountain-car benchmark solved without reward, value, or utility
interpretations: free-energy principle gives a unified account of action and perception
results invite a reappraisal of dopamine's role beyond reward signalling
